description:
Advances: specled.verification

Under dash (ubuntu /bin/sh), 'set -m' in a tty-less script prints 'can't access tty; job control turned off' and the backgrounded target never gets its own process group: the recorded pgid is not a group leader, kill -KILL -- -pgid is a no-op, and grandchildren survive the timeout. Empirically reproduced with /bin/dash on macOS. This is why verifier_test.exs:1004 (command timeout SIGKILLs the whole process group, scenario specled.verify.scenario.command_timeout_kills_process_group) fails in GitHub Actions while passing on macOS bash-as-sh. Fix: prefer setsid when available (universal on Linux) with set -m as the macOS fallback.
title: verification timeout group-kill is broken on Linux (dash rejects set -m)
